arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The key frames are #01 through #06 which are in `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

Microsoft publishes symbol files for .NET runtime builds on their Symbol Server, and I can use the BuildId to look up the corresponding debug symbols there or through dotnet symbol download tools.

The Symbol Server uses a specific URL format for ELF files based on the BuildId, so I can construct the direct download URL for either the library itself or its debug file. Let me try fetching from the Microsoft Symbol Server directly using this BuildId.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-e56b81049c3743839134f2e75853baa3 && mkdir -p symbols && BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801" && \
echo "Trying Microsoft Symbol Server..." && \
curl -sS -L --fail -o symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1 && echo "Downloaded debug file" || echo "Debug file download failed, trying alternate URL..." && \
curl -sS -L --fail -o symbols/libcoreclr.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" 2>&1 && echo "Downloaded symbol file" || echo "Symbol file download failed"
arguments.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft Symbol Server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft Symbol Server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available. Now I need to download the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

Let me try downloading from the Microsoft Symbol Server. The URL patterns for ELF files are:

1. `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so` (the binary itself with embedded symbols)


2. `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so` (the symbol file)
3. `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/_.debug` (the debug info)

I'll need to use the lowercase hex buildid without any prefix in these URLs. I could also try using the `dotnet-symbol` tool, but let me start by testing the Microsoft symbol server directly with curl.

echo "Trying Microsoft Symbol Server..." && \
curl -sS -L --fail -o symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1 && echo "Downloaded debug file" || echo "Debug file download failed, trying alternate URL..." && \
curl -sS -L --fail -o symbols/libcoreclr.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" 2>&1 && echo "Downloaded symbol file" || echo "Symbol file download failed"

arguments.command:
# Try debuginfod approach
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
for server in "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols"; do
  code=$(curl -sS -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${server}/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "${code} - ${server}/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo"
done

data.reasoningText:
The `dotnet-symbol` tool expects actual binary files as input, not URL patterns. It needs the actual `libcoreclr.so` binary to find and download symbols for it.

Since I don't have the actual `libcoreclr.so` binary and the BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801` appears to be fabricated (not found on Microsoft's symbol server), I cannot symbolicate these frames.

Let me try one more thing - use `debuginfod` which is a newer approach for fetching debug symbols. The `debuginfod` client can query servers by BuildId.


Actually, that BuildId really does look like a test value rather than something from an actual .NET runtime release. Even if I try the debuginfod protocol, which works purely off BuildId, I'm unlikely to find anything for a fabricated identifier.

arguments.command:
# Try NuGet-based approach: search for the coreclr runtime package for android-arm64
# The BuildId might correspond to a specific .NET version; let's try known .NET 8/9 android runtime packages
# First, let's try to find the symbol package directly
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try the dotnet symbol server with the SSQP protocol format (SymbolChecksum)
for fmt in \
  "libcoreclr.so/${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  "libcoreclr.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libcoreclr.so" \
  "libcoreclr.so.dbg/${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"; do
  code=$(curl -sS -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${fmt}" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "${code} - ${fmt}"
done

# Also try the nuget symbol server
for fmt in \
  "libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  "libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"; do
  code=$(curl -sS -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${fmt}" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "${code} (nuget) - ${fmt}"
done

All attempts to find the symbols on public symbol servers have failed. The BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801` is not found on any known symbol server. This is likely a test/fabricated tombstone file.

I should let the user know that I can't symbolicate because the BuildId doesn't match any publicly available .NET runtime symbols. I'll provide guidance on how they could symbolicate it themselves.

data.content:
I was unable to symbolicate the frames.
